hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 35th percentile — HPS (447) sits well under the Top 100 median (1359), but HPM (5.36) actually beats the Top 100 average (4.12) and sits close to the #1 parse (6.47). This points to an efficiency story, not a throughput one: Crowns is casting cheaper, mana-efficient heals (Flash of Light, 89.9% of output — see section 02) at a pace the Top 100 field doesn't match, just producing less raw output per second than the Holy-Light-heavy parses that top this boss.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light dominates at 89.9% of output, while the Top 100 sample spreads its healing across five different Holy Light ranks (guids 27136, 25292, 10329, 10328, 27135) that together average 92.6% — none of which Crowns cast this kill. A rare guid-1042 Holy Light entry (3.8%) is a different rank entirely, absent from the Top 100 sample on this boss.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock cast twice this kill (Coday, Jotes) — above the Top 100's roughly-once-every-four-fights norm (26% usage) — but Divine Favor (67% typical usage) and Mana Potion (82% typical usage) both went unused, the two clearest cooldown gaps on this kill. Zero deaths, so no death/cooldown timing to check.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (15.6%) sits dramatically below the Top 100 average's top target (88.3%) — the widest targeting gap of the raid night. This reads as broad, raid-wide healing assignment (17 distinct targets) rather than the tank-focused style that dominates this boss's Top 100 sample, not a targeting problem.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 68th percentile despite raw HPS (660) sitting well under the Top 100 median (1320) — HPS and percentile aren't computed the same way (this page's HPS is a straight event-sum, WCL's percentile its own points-based ranking), so the gap between them isn't a contradiction. Active time (84.3%) is the real soft spot, meaningfully below the Top 100 average (90.9%).
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Same Flash of Light lean seen all raid (57% here vs. the Top 100 average's 14.2%), though less extreme than Hydross — Crowns does cast the Top 100's dominant Holy Light rank here (guid 27136, 37.8% vs. the benchmark's 64.7%), just less of it.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Three real deaths this kill (Captinspanky, Vajony, Hatchpeppers, all within a 97-second window) — the only cooldown Crowns cast, a self Holy Shock, landed 17 seconds before the first of them and can't be read as a missed save for the other two, both well after. Hand of Protection (3% typical usage) and Divine Shield (26% typical usage) both went unused, but neither is common enough in the Top 100 sample for their absence to be a real deviation.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (14.7%) is well below the Top 100 average (61.1%) — the same broad-spread pattern as every other kill this raid — and overall coverage (56.6%) trails the benchmark's 84.5% too.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 74th percentile on the raid's deadliest kill — 8 real deaths, the most of any boss tonight. HPM (4.6) is the one kill this raid where mana efficiency falls slightly below the Top 100 average (5.08) rather than above it, and the spell mix here (66.2% Holy Light, 31.8% Flash of Light) is the closest match to the Top 100's own mix of any boss tonight — the raid's usual Flash-of-Light lean is weakest here.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The closest match to the Top 100's own spell mix of any boss this raid — Holy Light (66.2%) and Flash of Light (31.8%) both land within about 5-17 points of the benchmark average (70.8%/14.9%), a real narrowing of this raid's usual Flash-of-Light lean.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The raid's deadliest kill shows real, active cooldown use to match: Holy Shock, Divine Favor, Divine Shield, and Hand of Protection were all cast at least once. Divine Shield (self) sits within about 2-15 seconds of four of the eight deaths (Captinspanky, Vinnyvoss, Destromental, Hez) — since it's self-only, none of those four timings represent a missed save for anyone else. Hand of Protection landed on Churbert well outside the window of either of his own two deaths this kill (17s before his first, 53s after his second).
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (13.5%) is the closest to the Top 100 average of any kill this raid (20.6%, a 7.1-point gap, near the edge of normal variance) — this boss's own Top 100 sample is itself less tank-concentrated than most others, narrowing the usual gap.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 75th percentile, with overheal (19.6%) genuinely better than the Top 100 median (30.4%) and HPM (7.13) well above the Top 100 average (4.74) — one of the stronger efficiency showings of the raid, even though raw HPS (875) still trails the median (1589).
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light (69.4%) dominates again, far above the Top 100 average (9.9%) — one of this raid's more extreme Flash-of-Light kills, second only to Solarian and Hydross.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Three real deaths this kill — Shlicktree's and Deepbowlz's both land 21-32 seconds after a self Divine Shield (self-only, so not a missed save for either), while Danceswtrees's death sits 46 seconds from the nearest cooldown, Divine Favor, too far to read as related. Cleanse (3% typical usage) and Hand of Protection (2% typical) both went unused, but neither is common enough in the Top 100 sample for that to be a real gap.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (31.4%) trails the Top 100 average (69%) by a wide margin, though this is Crowns's highest top-target share of the raid alongside Morogrim — still a broader spread than the benchmark's tank-focused norm on this boss.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 4th percentile is the clear low point of the raid — the next-lowest kill is 35th (Hydross). Raw HPS (338) sits at just 28% of the Top 100 average (1222), the largest output gap of any boss tonight, while HPM (4.69) is close to the Top 100 median (4.66) — a real throughput shortfall specific to this kill, not a mana-efficiency problem.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Consistent with the rest of the raid, Flash of Light (63.5%) outweighs the Top 100 average (16.7%) by a wide margin. The same rare guid-1042 Holy Light entry seen on Hydross and Alar shows up again here (11.7%) — those two bosses' Top 100 benchmarks don't carry it at all, but this boss's benchmark now shows a small, real trace of it too (0.5%).
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Zero real deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death correlation to check. Holy Shock, Divine Favor, and Divine Shield were all cast once each (self every time), broadly in line with this boss's Top 100 usage rates (57%, 81%, 45% respectively). Mana Potion is the one real gap — unused here against a 95% typical-usage norm, the largest single-cooldown deviation of the kill. The real story, though, is throughput and active time (72%, the lowest of the raid alongside Alar and Kael'thas) — see the scorecard above.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (32.1%) — Crowns's highest of the raid alongside Karathress — still sits well under the Top 100 average (67.5%). Coverage (69.9%) is closer to the benchmark (86.9%) than on most other kills.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 85th percentile, the second-best of the raid. Overheal (23.8%) and HPM (7.69) both sit close to or above the Top 100 norm on this long, 498-second kill. Four of the fight's five deaths land within a 20-second window early in the pull — a real cluster worth noting, though not one this healer's own cooldown timing can be tied to.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark is already unusually Flash-of-Light-leaning on this boss (54.2%, the highest of any boss this raid) — Crowns pushes further still (88.9%), the widest raw percentage-point gap of the raid (34.7 points).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Two real cooldown gaps stand out: Divine Shield (57% typical usage) and Holy Shock (72% typical usage) were both left uncast this kill — the two largest single-cooldown deviations on this boss, Divine Shield specifically worth flagging given this fight's real chain-damage mechanics. Cleanse, by contrast, was used heavily and well-targeted (7 casts across 6 different raiders — Whitewolf, Pepsiweaving, Paladanwood, Churbert twice, Lippies, Turkeykin — vs. a Top 100 average of 1.8 casts), the strongest real cooldown usage of the raid. All four of the early death cluster (Purehentai, Destromental, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss) sit 35-55 seconds before Crowns's Cleanse casts began — too far to read as a response to any one of them; Captinspanky's later death is 23 seconds before a Mana Potion, also not a plausible correlation.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (13.1%) and overall coverage (47.9%) both land close to the Top 100 average (18% / 50.3%) — the smallest targeting gap of the raid, on a boss whose Top 100 sample itself spreads healing more broadly than most (the lowest benchmark top-target share of any boss this raid).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 40th percentile — HPS (399) sits at roughly 39% of the Top 100 average (1013), one of the wider output gaps of the raid, though HPM (5.92) is still above the Top 100 average (5.24), continuing this raid's pattern of mana-efficient but lower-throughput healing.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light (68.3%) again outpaces the Top 100 average (24.8%) — consistent with every other kill this raid.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Two real cooldown gaps on this kill: Holy Shock (74% typical usage) and Mana Potion (96% typical usage) both went unused — Mana Potion's absence is a large, real deviation given how close to universal its usage is in the Top 100 sample here. Zero real deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death correlation to check.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (13.6%) trails the Top 100 average (37.6%) by a wide margin, continuing this raid's broad-spread pattern.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 90th percentile — the best of the raid alongside Solarian. Active time (98.5%) is the highest of any kill this raid, and HPM (6.7) sits comfortably above the Top 100 average (5.44). Six real deaths this kill (the most of any boss besides Leotheras), including Churbert and Purehentai dying at the exact same timestamp.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light (55.5%) vs. the Top 100 average (10%) — the smallest raw Flash-of-Light/Holy-Light split within Crowns's own mix of any kill this raid (55.5% vs. 44.5% Holy Light), though the underlying lean over the benchmark is the same as every other kill. Crowns cast no Holy Shock this fight, matching the Top 100 sample's own near-negligible use of it here (0.6%).
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Six real deaths this kill. Banepain's and Raezy's deaths land 8-11 seconds after a Mana Potion (not a heal, so not a plausible save); the simultaneous Churbert/Purehentai death and Sackajaweeba's death both sit within 9-36 seconds of a Divine Favor cast, but Divine Favor is self-only here too, so none of these read as missed-save timings. None of this kill's utility cooldowns (Hand of Protection, Divine Shield, Cleanse) are common enough in the Top 100 sample here (6%, 12%, 0% usage) for their absence to read as a missed opportunity. Active time (98.5%) and HPM (6.7) are the real strengths of this kill.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (11.4%) and overall coverage (43.5%) both trail the Top 100 average (26.3% / 67.6%) noticeably — the most target-diffuse kill of the raid by distinct-target count.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: 92nd percentile, the best kill of the raid. HPM (7.4) is nearly double the Top 100 average (4.53) — the most mana-efficient kill of the raid — even though raw HPS (786) still trails the median (1482). Spell mix here is the most extreme expression of this raid's Flash-of-Light lean: 98.2% of output, against the Top 100 average's Holy-Light-dominant mix.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The most extreme expression of this raid's Flash-of-Light lean: 98.2% of Crowns's output this kill, against a Top 100 average that's still Holy-Light-dominant (71.1%) — Crowns casts almost no Holy Light at all on this particular boss.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Zero real deaths this kill. Holy Shock's single cast landed on Captinspanky rather than self — a genuinely other-targeted use, consistent with 97% of this boss's Top 100 sample casting it on someone other than themselves (self%: 3%). This is the raid's best kill by both percentile (92nd) and mana efficiency (HPM 7.4).
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (11.2%) and coverage (42.9%) both trail the Top 100 average — consistent with the raid-wide broad-spread pattern, on the kill where Crowns's spell mix is also the most extreme (see section 02).

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: 74th percentile on the raid's longest kill (476s) and lowest active-time reading (68.5%, vs. the Top 100 average of 83.1%) — four real deaths this kill, including Deepbowlz and Vinnyvoss dying at the exact same timestamp. HPM (5.66) is essentially even with the Top 100 average (5.61), a rare kill this raid where mana efficiency isn't a clear relative strength.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light (74.3%) still leads, but the gap narrows here because the Top 100 average itself leans more toward Flash of Light on this boss (35.2%, the second-highest benchmark Flash-of-Light share of the raid after Lady Vashj) than on most others.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Four real deaths this kill. Divine Shield (self) was cast about 1.4 seconds after Banepain's death — most plausibly a reactive self-preservation cast under raid-wipe pressure, not something that could have prevented that specific death (Divine Shield only protects the caster). Sackajaweeba's death sits 10 seconds before a Mana Potion; the simultaneous Deepbowlz/Vinnyvoss death is 96 seconds from the nearest cooldown, too far to read as related. Holy Shock (82% typical usage) also went unused this kill, a real gap on the raid's longest, most active-time-starved fight.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (16%) and coverage (47.6%) both trail the Top 100 average (25.8% / 63.3%), consistent with every other kill this raid — the broad-spread pattern holds even on this, the raid's longest fight.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: identical across 8 of 10 kills, with two single-kill equipment swaps (Lurker, Void Reaver — see below)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Ring enchants aren't flagged, per this era's self-only Enchanting restriction. No belt enchant exists in this ruleset either, so its absence isn't a gap. All 9 enchantable slots carry a real permanent enchant on every kill — the only real gear findings this raid night are the two deliberate, single-kill equipment swaps below, not omissions.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Morogrim Tidewalker is a dramatic single-kill outlier — 4th percentile, the lowest of the raid by a wide margin (the next-lowest is Hydross at 35th) — driven by raw HPS sitting at just 28% of the Top 100 average on that kill (338 vs. 1222), the largest single-kill output gap of the raid, despite HPM there (4.69) matching the Top 100 median almost exactly. Separately, target-distribution concentration sits well below the Top 100 average on 8 of 10 kills — a consistent, raid-wide pattern of broader healing assignment than the tank-focused style common among Top 100 parses, not a targeting problem, and consistent with Crowns's raid-wide lean on Flash of Light over Holy Light.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: 7 of 10 kills land at or above the 68th percentile (Lurker, Leotheras, Kael'thas, Karathress, Lady Vashj, Void Reaver, Solarian); Hydross (35th) and Al'ar (40th) are real, if less severe, soft spots, and Morogrim (4th) is the standalone low point flagged above. HPM sits at or above the Top 100 average on 6 of 10 kills outright, and close to even on the remaining 4 — no kill this raid shows HPM meaningfully below the benchmark, a genuine relative strength running through the whole raid. All 10 percentiles are exact report+fight WCL rankings, not fuzzy-matched.

GearCheckItems.Description: Mainhand and offhand were swapped for a Fishing Pole (no offhand) on The Lurker Below only
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, benign swap, not a gearing gap — every other kill (Hydross through Kael'thas Sunstrider, all but Lurker) equips the standard mace (item 28771) + shield (item 29458) combo; Lurker alone shows a fishing pole in mainhand with no offhand equipped, confirmed from real combatantinfo snapshots on both sides of the swap, consistent with fishing during that specific pull.

GearCheckItems.Description: Trinket (second slot) was swapped for a different real trinket on Void Reaver only
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Every other kill carries the same real trinket (item 28590); Void Reaver alone shows a different real trinket (item 28727) equipped instead — a deliberate per-encounter swap, not a gearing gap.
